goal: Approve registry/muscle_compliance_check.py as an Apex tool: review the code, allocate the canonical ref code via doc_controller, run TC-001..TC-008, complete docs/tools/muscle_compliance_check/test_record.md with real evidence and the Approved YES stamp, add the tool to APEX_TOOL_MENU.json + manifest.json + DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md + TOOL_INDEX.md, regenerate APEX_TOOL_MENU.md, and confirm validate_tool_docs --quiet still returns OK.
requester: Claude (Opus 4.7, GPU-RTX5090, doc author per operating contract)
assigned_to: Codex (code review + test execution + index/registry mutations per operating contract)

forbidden_actions: Promote any OTHER batch-#2..#5 artifacts in this packet — promotion is one batch at a time per playbook
Bulk-merge other parallel-Claude WIP files outside the muscle_compliance_check scope
Edit muscle_compliance_check.py beyond the header Ref:* line update unless a TC failure requires a fix (then attach the diff in the Task Result)
Expose or write plaintext secrets (this is the very thing the tool detects)
Perform destructive action without backup and rollback

input_files: registry/muscle_compliance_check.py — the source under review (711 lines, stdlib only)
docs/NAMING_CONVENTION.md — ref-code format + 3-doc rule + approval gate
docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.json — current approved tool catalogue (8 tools)
docs/tools/muscle_compliance_check/test_record.md — STUB drafted by Claude; Codex fills with real results
registry/validate_tool_docs.py — must still return OK after this batch
registry/generate_tool_menu.py — used to regenerate the human menu
write_scope: docs/tools/muscle_compliance_check/test_record.md — REPLACE stub with real test evidence + Approved stamp
registry/muscle_compliance_check.py — header Ref: line ONLY (APEX-SYS-PY-00001 → real allocated code); also fix anything TC-001..TC-008 reveals as broken, with diff attached
docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.md — REGENERATE from JSON via generate_tool_menu.py (do not hand-edit)
docs/DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md — add row with allocated ref code, file path, description, ACTIVE state
registry/manifest.json — add or refresh entry (per approve_tool.py behaviour)
registry/TOOL_INDEX.md — REGENERATE per approve_tool.py / indexer policy
audit/compliance/ — natural side effect of running TC-002 etc.; commit only the latest evidence JSON referenced from test_record.md
hub/WO-APEX-PROMOTE-COMPLIANCE-CHECK-001.json — append a `status` field with COMPLETED/BLOCKED + completion timestamp + result link
constraints: Make the smallest safe change that completes the task — do NOT refactor the muscle for style.
Follow the existing tool-doc convention exactly (mirror muscle_resource_guard's format).
Use doc_controller.py to allocate the ref code, not a hand-typed slot. The proposed APEX-MB-PY-00028 is a starting point only.
Idempotency: rerunning the promotion steps should not double-write register rows or menu entries.
No secrets in any committed artifact. (The tool flags them — don't let the promotion be ironic.)
Run validate_tool_docs --quiet after EACH register/menu edit, not just at the end.
Codex review the source code in addition to running tests — flag any concerns about the regex patterns, the stale-path remap behavior, or the secret-detection scope BEFORE approval.

files: audit/compliance/<UTC-timestamp>.json (latest run kept as test evidence)
expected_output.report: TASK RESULT containing: status (OK|BLOCKED|FAIL), allocated ref code, files changed (with diffs for any source edits), all TC commands run with exit codes and JSON output paths, validate_tool_docs final result, link to evidence JSON, risks remaining, rollback instructions, recommended next batch (which should be #2 claude_codex_loop).

test_command: python registry/validate_tool_docs.py --quiet && python registry/muscle_compliance_check.py --quiet && python registry/muscle_compliance_check.py --quiet --strict || echo "see report for detail"
success_criteria: All 8 TCs in test_record.md have actual results filled in (no remaining `_pending_` values).
`python registry/muscle_compliance_check.py --quiet` returns exit 0 (or exit 1 with documented FAILs that are pre-existing and unrelated to this promotion).
APEX_TOOL_MENU.json has a muscle_compliance_check entry under approved_tools[] AND it's removed from pending_tools[] if present.
APEX_TOOL_MENU.md regenerated from JSON (do NOT hand-edit) and the file mtime is later than the JSON mtime.
DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md has a row keyed on the allocated ref code, path `apex/registry/muscle_compliance_check.py`, status ACTIVE.
Source file header `Ref:` line updated to the allocated ref code.
No secrets committed (verify with `python registry/muscle_compliance_check.py --quiet --strict` showing secrets_on_disk = 0).
rollback_plan: If approval cannot be completed: (1) git restore docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.json docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.md docs/DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md registry/manifest.json registry/TOOL_INDEX.md to undo registry mutations; (2) leave the four docs/tools/muscle_compliance_check/*.md in place (they're harmless DRAFTs); (3) leave registry/muscle_compliance_check.py header as-is (no need to revert the Ref: change); (4) append BLOCKED status + reason to this Task Packet; (5) escalate via the Task Result `blocker_report_fields`.
evidence_required: Exact commands run for each TC plus stdout / exit code (paste into test_record.md per template format).
Path to the latest audit/compliance/*.json proving TC-002 actually executed.
approve_tool.py output if it was run as the final gate.
Diff of any source edit beyond the header Ref: line.

stop_for: TC-001 syntax check fails (source has a parser error)
TC-005 missing-register graceful exit returns wrong code or hangs
TC-007 secret detection FAILS to find an obvious .env (positive control fails)
Allocated ref code collides with an existing register entry that isn't trivially renumberable
validate_tool_docs.py returns non-OK after the menu/register edits
blocker_report_fields: Exact blocker (which TC, which command, which file)
Why it blocks promotion (vs. being a pre-existing unrelated FAIL)
Lowest-friction resolution (e.g. source bug fix, register edit, doc clarification)
Safe fallback path (e.g. leave tool as DRAFT, absorb checks into validate_tool_docs.py per playbook option (b))
What can continue independently (e.g. batch #2 promotion of claude_codex_loop can start in parallel since it's a separate tool)

promotion_provenance.batch_in_playbook: Promote the compliance checker first if it remains useful.
promotion_provenance.playbook_option_chosen: (a) Approve as standalone tool — NOT (b) absorb into validate_tool_docs.py. Reason: distinct scope. validate_tool_docs.py is the narrow pre-commit gate; compliance_check is the broader audit including ref uniqueness, header/register agreement, register-path resolution, secret detection, three-doc rule, naming-format validation.

doctrine_notes: Per APEX_CONTEXT_INDEX.md operating contract: Claude owns architecture/docs/reviews; Codex owns code edits/tests/configs. This packet honors that split.

Per APEX_PARALLEL_MERGE_PLAYBOOK conflict rule: 'A WIP script is useful but undocumented → It remains WIP until approved docs and tests exist.' This packet provides the docs; Codex provides the tests.
Per user instruction 2026-05-25 13:53Z: 'Do not self-review around Codex where Codex review is required.' Claude must NOT mark this batch APPROVED on Codex's behalf.

codex_result.result_summary: Codex fixed recurring source defects in path resolution, approval stamp detection, missing-register exit handling, and external/stale path classification. Tool is not approved because it still reports real estate-level findings including secrets_on_disk, duplicate refs, header/register mismatches, stale/missing paths, and older undocumented muscles.
codex_result.safe_next_action: Run a separate Apex document-control cleanup batch before stamping this tool approved.
